name: Arsenic AND mercury gate (dual heavy-metal)
shortDescription: A two-input AND gate that fires only when BOTH arsenic and mercury are present — reducing false positives and flagging co-contaminated water. ArsR drives the sgRNA and MerR drives dCas9, so CRISPRi repression (and the readout) needs both metals.

sensing.sensorMechanism: ArsR de-represses Pars on arsenite; MerR activates PmerT on mercury.
sensing.signalFlow: Arsenite -> Pars drives the anti-reporter sgRNA. Hg(II) -> PmerT drives dCas9. Only when BOTH metals are present do dCas9 and sgRNA together repress the constitutive reporter (CRISPRi AND). With one or neither metal, the reporter stays ON.

parts.name: ArsR + Pars (input 1)
parts.source: E. coli ars operon; Stocker et al. 2003

parts.name: amilCP reporter
parts.notes: Constitutive; repressed only when both inputs present.

output.result: Pigment is lost only when both arsenic and mercury are present.

performance.notes: Both sensing modules and CRISPRi are validated separately; the AND-gate integration is a design template. Requiring two co-contaminants sharply cuts single-analyte false positives.

safety.biocontainment: Lab/contained; add kill-switch for field use.
safety.notes: E. coli K-12 non-pathogenic BSL-1 host.

buildSteps.title: Design the AND topology
buildSteps.description: Route input 1 (ArsR/Pars) to the sgRNA and input 2 (MerR/PmerT) to dCas9; check off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: ArsR + Pars -> sgRNA. TU2: MerR + PmerT -> dCas9. TU3: constitutive amilCP.

buildSteps.title: Transform E. coli K-12
buildSteps.description: Select; verify pigment-on with each metal alone, pigment-off only with both.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Test all four combinations (neither/As/Hg/both) plus standards.

buildSteps.title: Score colour
buildSteps.description: Loss of pigment = both metals present.

provenance.source: Design template: a two-input CRISPRi AND gate combining validated ArsR and MerR metal sensors
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
